summary: Excellent Menke audience recording with full‑throated bass, but the show is an average performance aside from a scorching Sugar Magnolia jam and solid Peggy‑O.
review: The tape captures crystal‑clear instrument separation and a booming low end that makes this Menke audience recording stand out technically. Musically the concert is viewed as solid yet unremarkable, with a few high points – notably an electrifying Sugar Magnolia segment and a well‑sung Peggy‑O – but generally lacking the usual spark of a great Dead show.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is praised as excellent, while the musical performance is judged average with only a few standout moments; weighted combination yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Full‑throated bass and prominent instruments throughout; crisp, well‑balanced sound typical of high‑quality Menke audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall an average set with solid playing but lacking the usual fire; a few peaks (the Sugar Magnolia jam) break up a generally steady, unremarkable flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals praised as strong on Peggy‑O, though some reviewers note occasional lapses in Garcia's playing and missing 'x‑factor'.
band_member_comments.Phil: His timing on the explosive Sugar Magnolia jam is highlighted as perfect.
